title: A bill to direct the Joint Committee of Congress on the Library to procure a statue of Benjamin Franklin for placement in the United States Capitol.
plain_summary: This bill tells Congress to get a statue of Benjamin Franklin made and put it in the Capitol building. The statue must be placed in a spot where visitors can see it when they take tours of the Capitol. Congress has to sign the contract to make the statue by the end of 2025. The statue must be put in place by the end of 2026.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.

sections.title: Section 1  Procurement and Placement of Statue of Benjamin Franklin in the United States Capitol
sections.text: By December thirty-first, two thousand twenty-five, a special group of Congress members called the Joint Committee of Congress on the Library must make an agreement to get a statue of Benjamin Franklin. They can set up this agreement however they think is best as long as they follow the law. By December thirty-first, two thousand twenty-six, this same group must put the statue in a good permanent spot inside the United States Capitol building. The statue must be placed where visitors can see it when they take official tours of the Capitol building that are run by the Office of the Capitol Visitor Center.
